risk_assessment.recommended_action_detail: Final criminal conviction for large-scale financial fraud places the subject in the highest risk category. Onboarding should be declined unless a formal exemption is signed off by senior compliance.
risk_assessment.summary: Bernard L. Madoff was convicted in 2009 of operating the largest Ponzi scheme in history. He pleaded guilty to 11 felony counts including securities fraud, investment adviser fraud, mail fraud, wire fraud, and money laundering, and was sentenced to 150 years in prison.

distinct_signals.explanation: On 2009-03-12 Madoff pleaded guilty to all eleven felony counts in the Criminal Information without a plea agreement, admitting his role in a decades-long, multi-billion dollar Ponzi scheme. On 2009-06-29 Judge Denny Chin sentenced him to 150 years in prison.

distinct_signals.tag: SEC civil enforcement action — partial judgment
distinct_signals.explanation: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission filed a civil enforcement action and obtained an emergency asset freeze. On 2009-02-09 Madoff consented to a partial judgment imposing a permanent injunction.

timeline_summary.label: Federal arrest
timeline_summary.description: Bernard L. Madoff was arrested in New York on a federal criminal complaint alleging securities fraud.

timeline_summary.label: Guilty plea
timeline_summary.description: Madoff pleaded guilty to all eleven felony counts in the Criminal Information.

timeline_summary.label: Sentencing
timeline_summary.description: U.S. District Judge Denny Chin sentenced Madoff to 150 years in prison.

individuals.name: Bernard L. Madoff
individuals.extract: Founder of Bernard L. Madoff Investment Securities LLC. Convicted in 2009 of operating the largest Ponzi scheme in history.

organizations.name: Bernard L. Madoff Investment Securities LLC
organizations.extract: Investment advisory firm at the heart of the Ponzi scheme. Subject to SEC asset freeze and SIPA liquidation.

organizations.name: U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC)
organizations.extract: Filed civil enforcement action; obtained partial judgment with permanent injunction.

key_topics.summary: Decades-long, multi-billion-dollar fraudulent investment scheme using earlier investors' funds to pay later investors.
Material misrepresentations and omissions in connection with the purchase or sale of securities.
Three counts of money laundering charged in the criminal information.

edd_triggers: Final criminal conviction for large-scale financial fraud
SEC enforcement action with permanent injunction

sources_reviewed.summary: DOJ case page documenting the criminal information, plea, sentencing and victim compensation.
SEC litigation release announcing the partial judgment imposing a permanent injunction.
